items.headline: NBA Highlights: Lakers 119, Warriors 103
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Lakers 119, Warriors 103 — LeBron James had 26 points, 11 assists and eight rebounds, Deandre Ayton added 21 points and the Los Angeles Lakers picked up a 119-103 win over the Golden State Warriors.

items.headline: Jackie Young Becomes First WNBA Player To Sign Million Dollar Contract In Return To Aces
items.blurb: Jackie Young Becomes First WNBA Player To Sign Million Dollar Contract In Return To Aces — Four-time All-Star guard Jackie Young is finalizing a one-year agreement to return to the Las Vegas Aces worth $1.19 million, sources told ESPN Thursday, making her the first player in WNBA history to sign a million dollar contract. items.headline: Canada’s Leonard Miller has career night in win over Wizards
items.blurb: Canada’s Leonard Miller has career night in win over Wizards — Leonard Miller scored a career-high 26 points and matched his career best with 11 rebounds as the Chicago Bulls eased to a 119-108 victory over the Washington Wizards on Thursday night to sweep a two-game set.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Pacers 123, Nets 94
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pacers 123, Nets 94 — Micah Potter had 18 points and 14 rebounds, and Obi Toppin poured in 26 off the bench as the Indiana Pacers dominated the Brooklyn Nets 123-94.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Raptors 128, Heat 114
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Raptors 128, Heat 114 — Brandon Ingram poured in a season-high 38 points and RJ Barrett added 22 as the Toronto Raptors took down the Miami Heat 128-114.

items.blurb: NBA Clears Kings, Says Late-Game Foul On Seth Curry Was Coaching Error, Not Tanking — The NBA has cleared Sacramento Kings coach Doug Christie of tanking following an investigation into his decision to foul Golden State Warriors guard Seth Curry in the final minutes of Tuesday's 110-105 loss, determining the move was a tactical miscalculation rather than a deliberate effort to lose. items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Ruled Out For Bucks' Home Finale On Friday
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo Ruled Out For Bucks' Home Finale On Friday — Giannis Antetokounmpo has been ruled out by the Milwaukee Bucks for their regular season finale on Friday against the Brooklyn Nets. Antetokounmpo has been out since suffered a hyperextended left knee and a bone bruise on March 15th. items.headline: NBA Roundup: Knicks stay alive for No. 2 seed, beat Celtics in Tatum’s MSG return
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: Knicks stay alive for No. 2 seed, beat Celtics in Tatum’s MSG return — Josh Hart scored 15 of his 26 points in the fourth quarter, Jalen Brunson had 25 points and 10 assists, and the New York Knicks stayed alive in the race for the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 112-106 victory over Jayson Tatum and the Boston Celtics on Thursday night.

items.blurb: Wizards’ Sarr dealing with toe injury, ‘unlikely’ to return to lineup this season — Wizards forward Alex Sarr is likely done for the season due to a toe injury, Washington coach Brian Keefe said Thursday.

items.headline: 76ers’ Joel Embiid undergoes successful appendectomy
items.blurb: 76ers’ Joel Embiid undergoes successful appendectomy — Star centre Joel Embiid has been diagnosed with appendicitis and will undergo surgery on Thursday afternoon in Houston, the team announced.

items.headline: Joel Embiid To Undergo Appendectomy
items.blurb: Joel Embiid To Undergo Appendectomy — Joel Embiid has been diagnosed with appendicitis and will undergo surgery this afternoon in Houston. Embiid has appeared in 38 games this season, averaging 26.9 points, 7.7 rebounds and 3.9 assists in 31.6 minutes. Embiid has a PER of 24.5. NBA players have roughly needed three weeks to recover from an appendectomy before returning to game action.

items.headline: Magic Fined $25,000 Over Anthony Black Injury Reporting
items.blurb: Magic Fined $25,000 Over Anthony Black Injury Reporting — The NBA announced today that the Orlando Magic organization has been fined $25,000 for violating league injury reporting rules. The Magic failed to accurately disclose the game availability status of Anthony Black prior to their game against the Detroit Pistons on April 6. items.blurb: LeBron James: Leads Los Angeles with 26 points — James collected 26 points (11-17 FG, 3-5 3Pt, 1-2 FT), eight rebounds, 11 assists, two steals and one block in 32 minutes during Thursday's 119-103 win over the Warriors. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
